notes:
A reliability, sync-correctness, and security release — the fixes from a
comprehensive multi-agent code audit (the closed `audit`-labelled issues
#155–#185).

**Fixes & polish**

- **Editing on two devices at once no longer loses changes or drifts apart.**
  Renaming a note on one device while bookmarking (or trashing) it on another now
  keeps *both* changes instead of silently dropping one — and a note you trashed
  no longer reappears on a peer. Reordering a bullet no longer overwrites a text
  edit someone made to it at the same time. Each device converges to the same
  result. (§#156, #157; the timestamp-ordering fix #167.)
- **Editing around indented lines is safe.** Backspacing an indented line into
  its parent no longer detaches its sub-items to the top level; pressing Delete
  at the end of a line now pulls the next line up (Logseq-style); and collapsed
  outlines stay intact while you edit around them.
- **Typing `[` or `(` around a link, tag, or image no longer deletes it.**
- **The same note open in two places can't clobber your edits anymore** — the
  copy you're not typing in refreshes instead of overwriting; and a stale
  Format/Edit menu command can no longer error after you navigate away.
- **Grayscale images import again**, and **images sync reliably across devices**
  without getting permanently stuck.
- **Imported code blocks stay verbatim** — `[[...]]` or `#tag` text inside code
  no longer creates stray notes or gets mangled — and a hugely-nested import no
  longer crashes the Markdown export.
- **Backing up is crash-safe**: an interrupted backup can no longer destroy the
  previous copy.
- **Import, Export to Markdown, and Back Up Now no longer freeze the app** — they
  run in the background, keeping the editor responsive.
- **Smaller fixes:** Trash/Archive tabs survive a restart; the "hasn't synced in
  a while" nudge respects your dismissal; pasted numbered lists stay numbered;
  turning off crash reporting takes effect immediately (not just next launch);
  and the Sync settings screen no longer crashes on a bad device timestamp.

**Under the hood**

- **Sync conflict resolution is now per-field / per-op** (note metadata, block
  content vs. order), so unrelated concurrent changes can't supersede each other.
- **Server storage quota is enforced at the authoritative write boundary** and
  can't be bypassed or raced (§Q8).
- **Feedback endpoint hardened** — server-side size caps, optional shared-secret
  auth, rate-limit hooks, and no internal error leakage.
- **Defense-in-depth & data-integrity**: image-blob hash validation + decode
  limits, bounded tree traversals (no runaway on a corrupted parent cycle),
  atomic/idempotent persistence, and a guard that keeps heavy I/O off the UI
  thread. Backed by new unit, live-server sync-simulator, and component tests.
